verdict.answer_first: B states the practical answer immediately: "so your data stays correct even when things go wrong"; A opens with a correct definition but delays why it matters.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits more cleanly with "Reach for it whenever incorrect data would actually hurt"; A is useful but more survey-like with "Want me to go deeper" and broader ACID vs BASE framing.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives the clear rule "It matters most when wrong data has real cost" and names common cases without over-expanding.
